librarian_exploration_summaries_received.original_query: What existing repo touchpoints should shape graceful-shutdown planning in rls-orders-cnsmr-api?
librarian_exploration_summaries_received.summary: Program.cs and GoogleCloudLoggingStartup.cs are bootstrap/shutdown surfaces; OrdersController and TestsController through OrderService/helpers/repositories are the primary deep-propagation chains.

librarian_exploration_summaries_received.original_query: What dependency order is safest for the implementation work?
librarian_exploration_summaries_received.summary: Use T1 -> T2 -> T3 -> T4 so shutdown wiring precedes deeper cancellation propagation and documentation closes out AC4.

decisions_made.rationale: It cleanly covers all five acceptance criteria without collapsing important dependencies or over-fragmenting the work.

decisions_made.decision: Carry dual field names in each task entry
decisions_made.rationale: The repo prompt and repo-local validator/evaluator currently disagree on task field names, so compatibility aliases reduce downstream breakage.

issues_encountered.resolution: Added id/ac_mapping/complexity and acceptance_criteria aliases while preserving the prompt-required task_id/acceptance_criteria_mapped/estimated_complexity fields.

notes: Iteration 2 revised the planning artifact to remain task-generator compliant while also satisfying the local validator/evaluator expectations surfaced during review.
